topic_title: The Judgment Framework — Q1: What is the cost of this being wrong?
verdict: PASS — all 6 checks passed. Cycle 1 failures fully resolved: (1) LO5 no longer defines or asks learners to describe 'acceptable error' — it reads 'Name the other two Judgment Framework questions (covered in topics 9.8 and 9.9) and explain why Q1 must come first'; (2) Implementation Step 6 is now a bare mention-and-defer pointer 'Acceptable error thresholds are covered in topic 9.10.' with no defining clause; (3) 'precision (7.8)' in Pattern 3 now carries an inline definition 'meaning 94 in every 100 posts it removes actually violated the policy'. No scope_creep violations remain. Required sections present and non-trivial. Citation density strong (~92% of key claims in sections 5–7 carry markers; all 3 resources cited at least once). No forward references detected. Word count ~2100 words, within the 1500–6000 acceptable corpus range. No beginner readability failures. Corpus is ready to ground downstream artifacts.

primary_resources_cited.detail: [1] (aicompetence.org — primary) cited in sections 4, 5.1, 5.2, 5.3, 5.4, 5.5, 6, 7.1, 7.2, 10. Fully covered.

citation_density.key_claim_density_estimate: ~92% of standalone factual assertions in sections 5, 6, and 7 carry citation markers. Exceeds the 60% threshold.

observations: Term 'sensitivity rate' (section 7, Pattern 1) is absent from formal concepts_introduced for any prior topic (all topics 1.x–8.x have empty concepts_introduced arrays — data gap). However: (a) it derives from earlier curriculum weeks 7–8 (topic 7.9 Recall = sensitivity), not a forward reference; (b) an inline definition is provided ('meaning it correctly identifies 87 in every 100 true abnormalities'). Not flagged.

Terms 'false positive' and 'false negative' (section 7, Pattern 3) are similarly absent from formal concepts_introduced but both are inline-defined ('wrongly removing a post (false positive)'; 'wrongly leaving a post up (false negative)') and derive from prior weeks. Not flagged.
Q2 (topic 9.8), Q3 (topic 9.9), and 'Acceptable error thresholds' (topic 9.10) appear as bare names with explicit topic pointers and no definitions — correct mention-and-defer form.
data_gap_note: concepts_introduced arrays are empty for all topics 1.1–8.7. Forward-reference checking for weeks 1–8 vocabulary is degraded but no plausible forward references identified.

length_sanity.range_applied: 1500–6000 (caller-specified acceptable range for any depth profile)
length_sanity.note: Corpus is below the deep-profile reading artifact range (5000–6000 words) but that range applies to the reading artifact, not the corpus. The corpus at ~2100 words is within the 1500–6000 corpus acceptability window. Soft observation: depth profile appears to be standard/deep given the topic complexity and Implementation + Real-World Patterns sections; corpus could be expanded further if a deeper reading artifact is planned, but this does not fail the corpus check.
